programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio and the team investigate the case of a Hollywood star whose body has been found in the boot of a car on a film set. Starring David Caruso.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a port authority officer reveals Parsa's possible whereabouts as the team continues the hunt for the terrorist intent on wiping out NCIS. Bishop reveals her past association with the quarry and suggests where she thinks he may be found, but the CIA has intercepted a call that places Parsa in a different location to Ellie's hunch. Mark Harmon and Emily Wickersham star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a dishonourably discharged marine is accused of beating a Japanese tourist to death, having already been linked with the rape of a businessman's daughter. He claims that he is in love with the girl he is alleged to have raped, and that the accusations being thrown in his direction are a fabrication initiated by her father.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Ryan and Krumitz are taken hostage during an operation to take down a complex heist at a jewellery store. programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry helps a woman who is being blackmailed, but after she's also accused of murder, he must prove she's innocent on both counts.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is stabbed to death after stepping out of her shower, becoming the latest victim of the Dead Rose Slasher. Gibbs is approached by his old friend and barber Frankie Dean, who shares his concern that his son Cameron may be the killer. He explains that a photo-fit released to the media resembles him, and the attacks stopped during a period coinciding with his three-year stint in the navy. Meanwhile, Tony has to take probationary agent Ned Dorneget under his wing.(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A karate student joins a gang to avenge those responsible for shooting his brother, and Walker attempts to restore order to the neighbourhood before Christmas.(n)

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Lindsay investigate the fatal shooting of singer Kid Rock's chauffeur, whose body has been found backstage by a fan. programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when a naval seaman and an infamous arms dealer are found dead near the Mexican border. Evidence suggests the men were trying to smuggle a wooden box into the US, but there is no sign of the container, nor any clue as to what could be inside. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Marine sergeant Teddy Lemere is killed by a sniper in Afghanistan just after his sniffer dog helps guide a young child out of a minefield. Back in the US, his widow Ruby tells Gibbs and the team that she had recently received a message from her husband saying he might have upset a few people. Abby retrieves a tiny shard of the sniper's bullet from Ted's helmet and finds that it was made in America.(n)

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A man's mistress is found murdered, adding a further unpleasant dimension to his already acrimonious divorce - and before the CSIs can begin their investigation, another body turns up. programme.desc: A doctor dies on board the USS Borealis during a heavy storm and when the ship is brought into Norfolk Naval Yard, Ducky's investigation reveals a telltale mark on the victim's carotid artery, suggesting he was injected with poison. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A childhood friend of Gibbs' dead daughter is stalked by a marine who recently returned from Iraq, and asks the agent for help. Memories of his murdered family lead him to make the case a priority, but he acts on his initiative and leaves HQ without his badge, making DiNozzo fear he is risking his job for a personal vendetta. Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly star.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A taxi driver and an admiral working at the Pentagon are found executed by the side of a road, but the agents have trouble ascertaining a motive - until they uncover links to a woman searching for her missing husband, a political refugee from Africa. Meanwhile, McGee makes a discovery about his new girlfriend. Starring Sean Murray, Michael Weatherly and Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Abby babysits a bright nine-year-old while the other members of the team try to locate the youngster's missing father - a survivalist wanted in connection with a case of robbery and murder. The search takes them deep into the wilderness - where a startling discovery awaits. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A traumatised marine escapes from a psychiatric unit, convinced he is still fighting in Iraq. The agents believe his erratic behaviour indicates post-traumatic stress disorder and try to calmly talk him round before restraining him. However, blood tests reveal high levels of steroids in his system - meaning someone is using him as a human lab rat. Starring Lauren Holly, Sean Murray and Cote de Pablo.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Muslim US Marine is found seemingly shot dead near a mosque that has been linked to an al-Qaeda recruiter. Ducky refuses to perform an autopsy out of respect for the man's religion, but Abby runs tests that reveal his demise was actually due to poisoning from a rare octopus venom. Meanwhile, McGee goes into the mosque intending to plant a concealed microphone - but discovers someone has had the same idea.(n)

programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. programme.title: As Time Goes By

programme.title: As Time Goes By

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Detective Inspector Mike Shepherd is sent to the New Zealand town of Brokenwood to investigate the death of a farmer, after his body is found by two local fishermen. As he uncovers a family's tragedies and secrets, he also learns that Brokenwood is a place where shadows lurk just beneath the surface. Drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson.(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: After being released from jail, notorious paedophile John Davies admits to the abduction and murder of a child 25 years previously. Suspicious of his motives, the detectives reopen the unsolved case in an unusual attempt to prove his innocence, reinvestigating the boy's disappearance while on a demonstration with his left-wing activist parents. Crime drama, guest starring Adrian Schiller and Samantha Bond.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Norwich City v West Ham United at Carrow Road and Sheffield United v Chelsea at Bramall Lane. The clash between Norwich and West Ham could prove crucial in the battle to avoid the drop, while Chelsea were looking to further enhance their chances of playing Champions League football next term with victory over the Blades. Plus, Watford v Newcastle United, Liverpool v Burnley and Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester City.(n)

programme.title: Alan Hansen: Player and Pundit
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents a look at the respective careers of the ex-Liverpool star, who is soon to retire from his role at the BBC, which has seen him serve as an expert analyst since 1992. As a player he represented Liverpool in over 400 appearances between 1977 and 1991, during a glittering era that saw the club win eight league titles and three European Cups, among other honours. Hansen was also a member of the team during the infamous disasters at Hillsborough in 1989 and the Heysel Stadium in Belgium during the 1985 European Cup final, both of which he talks about candidly, revealing the impact they have had on him personally. After retiring as a player he went on to become one of the more respected TV analysts in the game, and was known for his passionate, forthright and intelligent views that led to him being referred to as the 'King of the Pundits' by his colleague Lineker, who is joined by the likes of Kenny Dalglish, Graeme Souness, Des Lynam, Gary Neville and Mark Lawrenson in.(n)

Gillian Burke explains how she has been helping with a survey of one of Cornwall's much-loved mammals, the grey seal.(n)

Emily feels totally outshone by Rosie's amazing schoolteacher and concentrates on developing her daughter's sign-language, while Simon worries that the other parents of special needs children look down on him. Things come to a head at the school sports day. Starring David Tennant, Jessica Hynes and Miley Locke.(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: New discoveries from the edge of the solar system are transforming what is known about Pluto, thanks to the New Horizons space probe that took the first-ever close up images of the planet. Pluto was once thought to be geologically dead, but the pictures revealed it to be an active world of stunning complexity, with mountains carved from ice, a nitrogen glacier that appeared to be moving and a recently active volcano, with data sent back lead some scientists to speculate that there may even be life on Pluto today.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches

However, the show's most successful entrant, Levi Roots, knows the best way to the Dragons' wallets is through their stomachs.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don checks the progress of the potatoes he planted earlier in the year and recommends plants for dry shade under trees. There are water-saving tips from a water scientist in Somerset who uses a variety of techniques to save as much water as possible in her own garden and advice from a gardener who has filled his home with over 1,000 houseplants. Plus, Nick Bailey on summer-flowering clematis and Ant Anderson meeting people planting up the Birmingham canal sides.(n)

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: Water has always been the driving force in East Anglia - its ebb and flow has shaped the fenland. The ancient wetlands once covered hundreds of square miles, but today, well over 90 per cent has been drained and converted into farmland. Every winter, thousands of whooper swans return to the flooded pastures of the Ouse washes from their Arctic breeding grounds. On the Welney Wetlands, spring sees 'mad' march hares boxing over mates. It was once thought these bouts were male hares boxing for dominance, but it is often the females throwing the punches to fend off the attentions of over-eager suitors. Narrated by Toby Jones.(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Actor Larry Hagman presents a look back at the highs and lows of pop culture in 1980, recalling the furore created by the on-screen shooting of his villainous character JR Ewing in Dallas. The programme also examines the success of Alan Parker's movie musical Fame, the emergence of electropop pioneers the Human League and OMD and the launch of arcade video game Pac-Man.(n)

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears. Wondering whether their marriage is running on empty, Louise and Tom meet prior to their first counselling session. With Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike.(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark provides comprehensive analysis of the day's national and international events.(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a compilation of the show's most passionate contestants, including a health food entrepreneur who confused the panel with the precise composition of his low carb noodles, a couple who withhold a vital piece of information while pitching their iced tea company, and a former boxer-turned-inventor.(n)

programme.title: Anita & Me (2002)
programme.desc: Twelve-year-old Meena strikes up an unlikely friendship with blonde teenage tearaway Anita. Despite their ethnic and social differences, the girls manage to support each other through difficult times while their families cope with life's problems. Comedy drama set in a Midlands village in the 1970s and based on the novel by Meera Syal, starring Chandeep Uppal, Anna Brewster, Max Beesley, Sanjeev Bhaskar, Kathy Burke, Lynn Redgrave and Ayesha Dharker.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Covid-19 has killed thousands, but now there are fears the pandemic has caused a crisis in cancer care that could mean many thousands more will die prematurely. Reporter Deborah James, who herself has incurable bowel cancer, explores the impact of the focus on coronavirus on cancer treatment. She investigates how the NHS has managed care during lockdown, speaks to experts and analyses new research, and also meets fellow patients to discover what the pandemic has meant for them.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: The vampires offer shelter to an old nemesis who is down on his luck.(n)

programme.title: What We Do in the Shadows
programme.desc: Nandor's old familiar returns to Staten Island and Nadja and Lazslo revive their musical.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.(n)

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: A fantasy-inspired wedding full of magical treats - especially for wedding planner and Wolfblood fan Amy who discovers star Leona will be helping her plan the even. Mum and Dad have to retrieve their rings from a tree stump infested with creepy crawlies and journey into an enchanted forest on unicorns before their fantasy wedding can magically appear.(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood investigate gruesome goings on, including a strange slime invading people's gardens, and a plant that pretends to be a dead animal.(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends explore a folk park in Omagh.(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The presenters celebrate the Purple Badge, with help from the Dengineers and gymnast Nile Wilson. Plus, the cast of Malory Towers join Mwaksy Mudenda to bake some tasty treats.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to Norway to dive with the deadliest predator of all: the orca.(n)

programme.title: Steve Backshall's Extreme Mountain Challenge
programme.desc: Part two of two. The naturalist reaches the peak of one of Venezuela's tepui mountains, before joining a party of Italian cavers as they venture into the unexplored caverns within. Inside, they discover strange wildlife and evidence of the geological forces that give the tepuis their unusual shape. Finally, the team returns to the ground by abseiling down the Angel Falls, the world's highest waterfall.(n)

programme.title: Big Cats
programme.desc: The second documentary looks at how cats have adapted to the challenges of life in almost every environment on Earth. The margay leaps through the treetops in the Central American jungle, while the pregnant jaguar waits for thousands of turtles to arrive on a remote Pacific beach. In the Karoo region of South Africa, a tiny black-footed cat stakes its claim to the title of the world's deadliest feline, and in Outer Mongolia, the Pallas's cat is able to disguise itself as a rock - a useful trick while hunting.(n)

programme.title: Wild West: America's Great Frontier
programme.desc: Wildlife in America's mountain regions, including the Rockies and the Sierra Nevada, areas infamous for extremes of temperature from freezing winters to scorching summers. The programme examines the survival strategies employed by animals including black bears, pikas and bison, as well as parasitic plants. Plus, a look at the challenges faced by these region's human inhabitants, including ranchers leading their cattle on 70-mile journeys.(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain heads to the West Country, picking fruit with jam maker Sarah Churchill and creating a mixed berry conserve to use in doughnuts. She also visits garlic farmer Mark Botwright and is put to work digging garlic bulbs, which inspires her recipe for apple walnut and coriander salad with burnt garlic dressing. Nadiya also puts her twist on local classics, with a tropical cream tea, and lamb and apple pasties.(n)

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Brian Turner accompanies astrologer Russell Grant as he visits places from his childhood, including the church where he was a choirboy and the flat where he lived with his parents.(n)

programme.title: The 39 Steps (1935)
programme.desc: A Canadian in London offers a safe hiding place to a British secret agent investigating a spy ring. However, when she is murdered, he realises he will be the prime suspect and goes on the run with the intention of exposing the real killers. Alfred Hitchcock's thriller based on John Buchan's novel, starring Robert Donat, Madeleine Carroll, Lucie Mannheim, Godfrey Tearle and Peggy Ashcroft.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Exploring the relationship between director Alfred Hitchcock and his stars, featuring interviews with the man himself, Kim Novak, Tippi Hedren, Joan Fontaine, Janet Leigh and Sean Connery. Sylvia Syms narrates.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: A journey around some of the most dramatic coastlines in Europe, uncovering the stories behind how these landscapes have shaped history.(n)

programme.title: Greatest Escapes to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman looks back at some of the most remote properties featured on Escape to the Country, as well as homes that offer the finest views. She also recalls rural businesses and activities that have taken presenters off the beaten track.(n)

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: Water has always been the driving force in East Anglia - its ebb and flow has shaped the fenland. The ancient wetlands once covered hundreds of square miles, but today, well over 90 per cent has been drained and converted into farmland. Every winter, thousands of whooper swans return to the flooded pastures of the Ouse washes from their Arctic breeding grounds. On the Welney Wetlands, spring sees 'mad' march hares boxing over mates. It was once thought these bouts were male hares boxing for dominance, but it is often the females throwing the punches to fend off the attentions of over-eager suitors. Narrated by Toby Jones.(n)

programme.title: Inside Central Station
programme.desc: Staff and passengers celebrate Glasgow Pride, students use the concourse as a location for a film, and signaller Brian explains how Victorian engineering keeps the network running.(n)

programme.title: A House Through Time
programme.desc: David Olusoga continues to chart British history through the successive inhabitants of a terraced house in Liverpool, focusing on the 1850s to the 1890s. David uncovers the stories of a widow who turned the dwelling into a boarding house, and an ambitious Jewish immigrant struggling to stay afloat in the midst of a financial crisis. He also traces the story of a seemingly successful couple for whom that house was the backdrop to a family drama involving violence and infidelity and reveals how an unidentified body dragged from the Mersey turned out to have surprising connections to the house.(n)

programme.title: Test Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents action from the fourth day of the First Test in the three-match series, held at Ageas Bowl in Southampton. With analysis from Michael Vaughan and commentary by Alastair Cook, Carlos Brathwaite, Phil Tufnell and Alison Mitchell.(n)

programme.title: British History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Historian Lucy Worsley debunks the so-called Glorious Revolution of 1688, when Dutch prince William of Orange invaded the British Isles and stole the throne from his father-in-law, King James II. It has since been described as a 'glorious' and 'bloodless' revolution. But given that it led to huge slaughter in Ireland and Scotland, how glorious was it really?(n)

programme.title: When Harvey Met Bob
programme.desc: Drama about the men who organised the Live Aid concerts in 1985. Having sold millions of copies of charity single Do They Know It's Christmas?, Boomtown Rats singer Bob Geldof realises much more still has to be done to combat famine in Ethiopia, and conceives the idea of a global music event - a plan that starts coming to fruition when he meets larger-than-life concert promoter Harvey Goldsmith. The film charts how their ambitious scheme turned into the biggest televised charity event in history, as well as documenting the many conflicts that sprang up between the unlikely partnership along the way. Starring Domhnall Gleeson and Ian Hart.(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Grace Jones presents a look back at a year when Live Aid took the world by storm, Prince and Bruce Springsteen topped the charts, and Miami Vice set the standard in men's fashion. Anarchic comic Viz hit newsagents' shelves nationwide and the second series of Spitting Image poked fun at the likes of Roy Hattersley and David Steel, who discuss how it felt to be on the receiving end.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Memorable hits of 1985, featuring TOTP performances spanning the genres. Performers include Wham!, the Smiths, Kate Bush, A-ha, Eurythmics, the Cure, Dead or Alive, Bonnie Tyler, Tears for Fears, Sister Sledge, Jennifer Rush and Doug E Fresh.(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar walks through the Bolton Abbey Estate, heading out from Bolton Priory along the River Wharfe and over moorland to her journey's end at Simon's Seat and its views across Wharfedale, Nidderdale and beyond. Meeting fellow walkers along the way, Shanaz finds inspirational spots to sketch and recite poetry, while discovering hidden woodlands and waterfalls.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a serene lakeside setting accented with an abundance of his trademark trees.(n)

programme.title: Tess of the D'Urbervilles
programme.desc: The death of Tess's father results in her family being forced out of their house, and she again finds herself at the mercy of Alec, who offers the Durbeyfields a home and support. Angel returns from Brazil and tracks down his wife, but his visit drives Tess to despair, and she lashes out at her benefactor Alec before spending one last night with Angel. Starring Gemma Arterton.(n)

programme.title: Borg vs McEnroe (2017)
programme.desc: During the summer of 1980, acclaimed Swedish tennis player Bjorn Borg is training for an unprecedented fifth consecutive Wimbledon men's singles title, with only one thing standing in his way - the brash and abrasive young American John McEnroe. A rivalry develops between the pair, culminating in one of the greatest matches of all time in that year's final. Biopic, starring Sverrir Gudnason, Shia LaBeouf and Stellan Skarsgard.(n)

programme.title: The History Boys (2006)
programme.desc: Eight unruly grammar school boys preparing to take their Oxbridge entrance exams are exposed to the contrasting teaching styles of two very different schoolmasters - the intellectually enthusiastic Hector, who seeks to instil a love of knowledge for its own sake, and the more cynical new arrival Irwin, who intends to equip them with the specific information they need to get ahead. Comedy drama adapted from Alan Bennett's play, with Richard Griffiths, Stephen Campbell Moore and Frances de la Tour.(n)

programme.title: Rebel Women: The Great Art Fightback
programme.desc: The story of the revolutionary feminist artists inspired by the cultural revolution of the 1960s, paying tribute to those that paved the way and examining their legacies. Featuring interviews with pioneers Judy Chicago, Margaret Harrison and Mary Kelly, as well as Lubaina Himid, Suzanne Lacy, Carolee Schneemann, Rose English, Laurie Simmons and Barbara Kruger.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross paints a serene lakeside setting accented with an abundance of his trademark trees.(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar walks through the Bolton Abbey Estate, heading out from Bolton Priory along the River Wharfe and over moorland to her journey's end at Simon's Seat and its views across Wharfedale, Nidderdale and beyond. Meeting fellow walkers along the way, Shanaz finds inspirational spots to sketch and recite poetry, while discovering hidden woodlands and waterfalls.(n)

programme.title: Ian Hislop's Stiff Upper Lip: An Emotional History of Britain
programme.desc: The host explores how the idea of a national character changed during the Victorian era, as suffering in silence came to be regarded as a noble trait - and even a form of civic duty. He visits Ardingly College in West Sussex, the boarding school where he was educated, to consider the benefits and drawbacks of an institutionalised approach to character-building, and talks to MP Rory Stewart, who discusses the emotional demands he faced as a diplomat in Iraq. Finally, he visits the First World War battlefields of the Somme, the place where he believes the flaws in the Victorian value system were brutally exposed.(n)

programme.title: Royal Opera House: The Re-Opening
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Katie Derham and Antonio Pappano introduce highlights of the Royal Opera House's first music and dance performances after lockdown, featuring a world premiere from choreographer Wayne MacGregor, classic opera arias and song performed by Gerald Finley, Louise Alder, Toby Spence, David Butt Philip, Sarah Connolly and the Royal Opera House's Jette Parker Young Artists, plus excerpts from ballets by Ashton, MacMillan and Wheeldon. As its music director, Antonio is joined by fellow musicians from the Orchestra of the Royal Opera House and the performances are filmed in line with current social distancing rules against the backdrop of the venue's auditorium.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 30 November 1989 and featuring performances by 808 State, Tina Turner, Kaoma, Tears for Fears, Kate Bush, New Kids on the Block and Gloria Estefan.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 7 December 1989 and featuring performances by Jason Donovan, Big Fun, Sydney Youngblood, Kate Bush, Soul II Soul, Dusty Springfield, Jeff Wayne, Latino Rave, Simple Minds and Queen.(n)

programme.title: Huey Morgan's Latin Music Adventure
programme.desc: After the Communist revolution of 1959, Cuba effectively closed its doors to the sights and sounds of the rest of the world. From this point, its music evolved in isolation from the other Latin speaking countries, with traditional forms placed at the heart of their sound. The rhythms and melodies of Cuba's people have captured the hearts of fans all around the world, and now that the government is gradually relaxing restrictions. Huey sets off from the capital Havana to explore the rich musical legacy of the island, as well as getting a taste of things to come.(n)

programme.title: Latin Music: A Session With
programme.desc: The award-winning, classically trained Cuban musician, singer and composer performs music inspired by her Afrocuban heritage. Including tracks from Cubafonia, the album that catapulted her onto the global stage.(n)

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: The making of Back to Black, the second and final studio album by singer-songwriter Amy Winehouse, which was released in October 2006 to wide critical and commercial acclaim. Featuring singles Rehab, You Know I'm No Good, Back to Black, Tears Dry on Their Own and Love Is a Losing Game, the record has sold approximately four million copies.(n)

programme.title: Better Than the Original: The Joy of the Cover Version
programme.desc: The role of the cover in the pop industry, investigating what it takes to reinvent someone else's hit and following the stories behind 10 cover songs, tracking how artists as varied as the Moody Blues, Soft Cell, Puff Daddy and Alexandra Burke have topped the charts with their retake on someone else's song. Contributors include John Cale, Gloria Jones, Marc Almond, Rick Rubin, Faith Evans and Nerina Pallot. Narrated by Meera Syal.(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the American art form from the mid-1980s to mid-1990s, an era that saw the success of 'New Traditionalists' such as Reba McEntire, George Strait and Dwight Yoakam. Garth Brooks exploded onto the scene to recalibrate the yardstick of success, while after being dropped from his label, an aging Johnny Cash, at the invitation of a hip-hop producer, returned to the studio with just his guitar and his unforgettable voice to record a series of albums that sold millions of copies and earned him the respect of the industry he helped to create. Contributors include Ricky Skaggs, Vince Gill, Willie Nelson, Rosanne Cash, Merle Haggard and Garth Brooks. Narrated by Peter Coyote.(n)

programme.title: Huey Morgan's Latin Music Adventure
programme.desc: After the Communist revolution of 1959, Cuba effectively closed its doors to the sights and sounds of the rest of the world. From this point, its music evolved in isolation from the other Latin speaking countries, with traditional forms placed at the heart of their sound. The rhythms and melodies of Cuba's people have captured the hearts of fans all around the world, and now that the government is gradually relaxing restrictions. Huey sets off from the capital Havana to explore the rich musical legacy of the island, as well as getting a taste of things to come.(n)

programme.title: Latin Music: A Session With
programme.desc: The award-winning, classically trained Cuban musician, singer and composer performs music inspired by her Afrocuban heritage. Including tracks from Cubafonia, the album that catapulted her onto the global stage.(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: On the Isle of Lewis in the Outer Hebrides, crofter Sandy Granville assesses the damage to his croft after one of the worst storms on record. Meanwhile, farmers in the north of Aberdeen and near Inverness scan their pregnant ewes, and on the West Coast, two hill farmers try to return four male tup lambs to a close friend. Plus, John Scott and Martine Irvine head to the Stirling Bull Sales in the hope of selling their best bulls.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: Documentary exploring some of the most inhospitable places on the planet concludes by shedding light on the lives of the Kamayura People, who reside in Brazil's Xingu Indigenous Park. For the Kamayura, pleasing the spirits is crucial to maintaining good health, and during one key festival, a warrior must dance for hours to appease a bird spirit, sustained only by food and drink brought to him by villagers. In Ethiopia, belief drives the residents of the Tigray region to climb a 400-metre mountain to reach their church. In Switzerland, the Zermatt air rescue team offers an insight into why millions of hikers, climbers and skiers visit the Swiss Alps every year, despite the risk of injury - or even death.(n)

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: The town of Vigata is shaken by the murder of a rich old man, and Montalbano must overcome a lack of concrete evidence as he investigates a mystery killer thought to be behind a series of animal shootings. In Italian.(n)

programme.title: Sicily: Wonder of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Michael Scott concludes his history of Sicily, telling the story of how the Christian Byzantine Empire was swept aside by a Muslim Arab invasion from North Africa. The island was transformed by new trade networks and agricultural techniques, and in the 19th century was unified with Italy - but the greatest threat to their prosperity then came from within and the rise of the Mafia.(n)

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Portillo heads to Gargnano to visit the romantic hideaway of DH Lawrence before ending his journey with a high-speed boat trip across Lake Garda.(n)

programme.desc: Dick and Dom head to the home of the skyscraper - New York City - to piece together the genius ideas that have seen buildings soar to incredible heights. programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC mates go on an adventure to find the most unusual and amazing places in Asia. programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are beside a river. They watch a fisherman make his rod longer and longer and longer. The fisherman casts out the line and then settles down for a nap. Chickedy and Chick run along the rod to the very end. Toodloo runs along the rod and jumps to join Chickedy and Chick. This causes the rod to spring up sending them flying! The fisherman wakes to discover a long, long, long fish at the end of his line. The Twirlywoo Screen draws a small line that Chickedy and Chick help make longer. Toodloo helps the line get a little longer still but when Great BigHoo taps the screen, the line becomes so long it shoots off the Twirlywoo Screen. The line gets longer and longer and goes into the Big Red Boat - right into Peekaboo's house! The Twirlywoos run after the Twirly Rings whilst Peekaboo reels in the long line.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of journalism, as Owen Jones, Paris Lees, Kay Burley, Adam Boulton, Carole Malone, Quentin Letts, Amol Rajan and Victoria Derbyshire take part.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade prepares to meet her birth mother for the first time. She's incredibly nervous, convinced she was given away because of her deafness, but Marty tries his best to reassure her. She faces her fears and heads to a cafe to meet her mum, finally uncovering the truth behind her past in the process. Meanwhile, Charlie throws himself back into work, but it's clear there is something else on his mind, and Faith attempts a conversation with Dylan but he pushes her away, claiming he needs some distance.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, rapper Tinie Tempah and comedian Katherine Ryan pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, Mo Farah takes on Jessica Ennis-Hill in The Sporting Fix, Peter takes Jamie Redknapp on an extreme retirement challenge and Ellie Goulding Saves Our Summer with an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Norwich City v West Ham United at Carrow Road and Sheffield United v Chelsea at Bramall Lane. The clash between Norwich and West Ham could prove crucial in the battle to avoid the drop, while Chelsea were looking to further enhance their chances of playing Champions League football next term with victory over the Blades. Plus, Watford v Newcastle United, Liverpool v Burnley and Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester City.(n)

programme.title: Alan Hansen: Player and Pundit
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents a look at the respective careers of the ex-Liverpool star, who is soon to retire from his role at the BBC, which has seen him serve as an expert analyst since 1992. As a player he represented Liverpool in over 400 appearances between 1977 and 1991, during a glittering era that saw the club win eight league titles and three European Cups, among other honours. Hansen was also a member of the team during the infamous disasters at Hillsborough in 1989 and the Heysel Stadium in Belgium during the 1985 European Cup final, both of which he talks about candidly, revealing the impact they have had on him personally. After retiring as a player he went on to become one of the more respected TV analysts in the game, and was known for his passionate, forthright and intelligent views that led to him being referred to as the 'King of the Pundits' by his colleague Lineker, who is joined by the likes of Kenny Dalglish, Graeme Souness, Des Lynam, Gary Neville and Mark Lawrenson in.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants are split into pairs and tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour - but there's a catch. They are separated by a wall, meaning the key to the task is communication as one pair recreates John Torode's pan-fried cod wrapped in ham and the other tackles his calf's liver and champ mash. For the second task, the competitors are sent out on their first mass catering challenge, at the McLaren Technology Centre in Woking, Surrey.(n)

programme.title: Alan Bennett's Talking Heads
programme.desc: Monica Dolan stars in The Shrine, the second of two new monologues written by Alan Bennett to sit alongside 10 of his classic TV scripts. Lorna visits the spot where Clifford's motorbike crashed, hoping for some closure. He had been such a love. But one day she finds someone else has left some flowers on the shrine.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: A busy night shift begins with Chelsey and Dave providing back-up for advanced paramedic Rob at a road traffic accident, where a driver has overturned his car at a roundabout. It is clear he has been drinking, meaning once they have assessed him, he will be answering questions from the police. Eric and Emily are dispatched to an 83-year-old who is in a lot of pain from her back, after which they answer a call from the police to a suspected assault on a 12-year-old, while senior paramedic Tony is called out to a man found slumped in his car in the city centre.(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Four-time World Superbikes champion Carl Fogarty, Great Britain sprinter Imani-Lara Lansiquot, former England striker Kevin Phillips and world Taekwondo champion Bianca Walkden join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the four remaining contestants battling for semi-final places, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - finalist and TV presenter Rylan Clark-Neal and champions Sophie Thompson and Kimberly Wyatt. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders push the boat out in a bid to secure their semi-final place.(n)

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Armchair fan Jack tells the story of the United Kingdom through its greatest sporting moments, as well as exploring themes such as loving the underdog and hating the Australians. Looking back on occasions such as the World Cup final of 1966 and 2012's Super Saturday, the comedian invites viewers to relive their most incredible sporting achievements while examining what its love of sport says about the nation.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: On the eve of Cathy's wedding, the bride-to-be cannot enjoy her pre-nup drink-up as she's obsessed by a scuff on the wall Marcus made when throwing her the car keys. Cat reluctantly agrees to drive Cathy to a DIY store to pick up some paint so they can cover the scuff but, at the store, they run in to Melanie, Cat's old school bully. Cathy decides to stand up for Cat and get the revenge Cat always wanted. Ellie White, Lauren Socha, Rebecca Front and Caroline Quentin star.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: Peter Kay and Sian Gibson return for a one-off special episode of the comedy about the daily commute of supermarket employees John and Kayleigh, with the two stars taking the plunge and improvising the dialogue for the whole show.(n)

programme.desc: Four-time World Superbikes champion Carl Fogarty, Great Britain sprinter Imani-Lara Lansiquot, former England striker Kevin Phillips and world Taekwondo champion Bianca Walkden join team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson and host Sue Barker on the quiz.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur John Torode and food writer and ingredients expert Gregg Wallace judge the four remaining contestants battling for semi-final places, tasking them to cook a faultless two-course meal. Lending their thoughts on proceedings are three legends of the series - finalist and TV presenter Rylan Clark-Neal and champions Sophie Thompson and Kimberly Wyatt. They know exactly what it takes to get through this competition and are almost certainly in for a treat as the contenders push the boat out in a bid to secure their semi-final place.(n)

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Armchair fan Jack tells the story of the United Kingdom through its greatest sporting moments, as well as exploring themes such as loving the underdog and hating the Australians. Looking back on occasions such as the World Cup final of 1966 and 2012's Super Saturday, the comedian invites viewers to relive their most incredible sporting achievements while examining what its love of sport says about the nation.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: On the eve of Cathy's wedding, the bride-to-be cannot enjoy her pre-nup drink-up as she's obsessed by a scuff on the wall Marcus made when throwing her the car keys. Cat reluctantly agrees to drive Cathy to a DIY store to pick up some paint so they can cover the scuff but, at the store, they run in to Melanie, Cat's old school bully. Cathy decides to stand up for Cat and get the revenge Cat always wanted. Ellie White, Lauren Socha, Rebecca Front and Caroline Quentin star.(n)

programme.title: The Paddy Raff Show
programme.desc: Comedy sketch pilot written and performed by the stand-up and social-media sensation. The backbone of the show is Paddy filming his own sketches at home, bottling his unique and hilarious observations of his family life and trying to deal with our 'new normal' of lockdown. Characters include Nigel the BT9 snob, Father Pat the raving priest and Granny Raff.(n)

programme.desc: Peter Kay and Sian Gibson return for a one-off special episode of the comedy about the daily commute of supermarket employees John and Kayleigh, with the two stars taking the plunge and improvising the dialogue for the whole show.(n)

programme.title: Rush (2013)
programme.desc: The 1976 Formula One Championship season is dominated by a fierce rivalry between two contrasting racers, as daredevil British driver James Hunt and methodical Austrian defending champion Niki Lauda put their lives on the line to outdo each other on the track. Fact-based drama, starring Chris Hemsworth, Daniel Bruhl and Olivia Wilde.(n)

programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Olia Hercules and Bryn Williams in the studio, and guest Martin Kemp via video link. Plus, archive clips and Helen McGinn chooses the wine.(n)

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond return with the series celebrating the best food Scotland has to offer, focusing on a different theme each week. They begin with Asian fusion, cooking up salt chilli squid with a Vietnamese dipping sauce. Joining them in the kitchen are chef Kaori Simpson from the Harajuku Kitchen in Edinburgh and actor Greg McHugh, who brings along his choice of craft beer to accompany the dish.(n)

programme.desc: Action from the event that saw 30 track and field stars representing USA, Europe and the Rest of the World compete across eight disciplines in different venues across the globe.(n)

programme.title: Wimbledon: The Greatest Championships
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents as a panel of experts select the best 10 ladies' finals of the Open era, featuring the likes of Serena Williams, Steffi Graf, Martina Navratilova, Chris Evert, Virginia Wade and Ann Jones.(n)

programme.title: Monsters University (2013)
programme.desc: Student creatures Mike Wazowski and James P Sullivan, aka Sulley, attend university to learn how to be scary, but their rivalry gets them both kicked off the course. The monstrous duo must learn how to work together if they are to realise their dreams of terrifying children. Winning the Scare Games will make the difference between Mike staying at the campus or having to leave. Pixar's animated comedy prequel to Monsters Inc, with the voices of John Goodman, Billy Crystal, Steve Buscemi and Helen Mirren.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys asks the questions as four more famous faces take to the black chair, with participants donating their fee to their chosen charities. Actress Bronagh Waugh, comedian Darren Harriott, TV presenter and campaigner Adam Pearson and presenter and author Riyadh Khalaf tackle questions on TV series Fleabag, American rapper Kanye West, World Wrestling Entertainment and James Cameron's movie Titanic.(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. As always, the challengers are tasked with coming up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, all the contestants come from the world of journalism, as Owen Jones, Paris Lees, Kay Burley, Adam Boulton, Carole Malone, Quentin Letts, Amol Rajan and Victoria Derbyshire take part.(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jade prepares to meet her birth mother for the first time. She's incredibly nervous, convinced she was given away because of her deafness, but Marty tries his best to reassure her. She faces her fears and heads to a cafe to meet her mum, finally uncovering the truth behind her past in the process. Meanwhile, Charlie throws himself back into work, but it's clear there is something else on his mind, and Faith attempts a conversation with Dylan but he pushes her away, claiming he needs some distance.(n)

programme.title: Peter Crouch: Save Our Summer
programme.desc: The former footballer hosts a variety entertainment show, featuring live music, comedy skits and a host of celebrity guests via video links. Peter will also be joined in each episode by TV and BBC Radio 1 presenter Maya Jama, as well as Taskmaster's Alex Horne and his comedy band the Horne Section. This time, rapper Tinie Tempah and comedian Katherine Ryan pop by for a chat and a game of Musical VAR, Mo Farah takes on Jessica Ennis-Hill in The Sporting Fix, Peter takes Jamie Redknapp on an extreme retirement challenge and Ellie Goulding Saves Our Summer with an exclusive performance in The Garden Sessions.(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Norwich City v West Ham United at Carrow Road and Sheffield United v Chelsea at Bramall Lane. The clash between Norwich and West Ham could prove crucial in the battle to avoid the drop, while Chelsea were looking to further enhance their chances of playing Champions League football next term with victory over the Blades. Plus, Watford v Newcastle United, Liverpool v Burnley and Brighton & Hove Albion v Manchester City.(n)

programme.title: Alan Hansen: Player and Pundit
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents a look at the respective careers of the ex-Liverpool star, who is soon to retire from his role at the BBC, which has seen him serve as an expert analyst since 1992. As a player he represented Liverpool in over 400 appearances between 1977 and 1991, during a glittering era that saw the club win eight league titles and three European Cups, among other honours. Hansen was also a member of the team during the infamous disasters at Hillsborough in 1989 and the Heysel Stadium in Belgium during the 1985 European Cup final, both of which he talks about candidly, revealing the impact they have had on him personally. After retiring as a player he went on to become one of the more respected TV analysts in the game, and was known for his passionate, forthright and intelligent views that led to him being referred to as the 'King of the Pundits' by his colleague Lineker, who is joined by the likes of Kenny Dalglish, Graeme Souness, Des Lynam, Gary Neville and Mark Lawrenson in.(n)

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: MP and peers on the National Security Strategy Joint Committee hear from the outgoing National Security Adviser Sir Mark Sedwill, from Wednesday 8 July.(n)

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: The statement in the House of Commons by the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak, on the economy including the announcement of a £2billion 'kick-start' scheme to subsidise six-month work placements for people on universal credit aged between 16 and 24 as well as a £3billion 'green' fund and boosts to apprenticeships, from Wednesday 8 July.(n)

programme.desc: Live coverage of a statement by Health Minister Nadine Dorries in response to the publication of the Cumberledge Review into medical device and medicine safety. programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Treasury select committee session on the economic impact of the coronavirus pandemic with evidence from Professor Hamish Lowe of Oxford University, former Permanent Secretary to the Treasury Lord Macpherson of Earl's Court and Bridget Rosewell of the National Infrastructure Commission, from Wednesday 8 July.(n)

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: The introduction to the House of Commons of a Bill under the ten minute rule on redundancy protection during pregnancy and maternity leave, from Wednesday 8 July.(n)

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Northern Ireland Adhoc committee on the coronavirus pandemic with a statement from the Communities minister, Carál Ní Chuilín, from earlier today.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Covid-19 has killed thousands, but now there are fears the pandemic has caused a crisis in cancer care that could mean many thousands more will die prematurely. Reporter Deborah James, who herself has incurable bowel cancer, explores the impact of the focus on coronavirus on cancer treatment. She investigates how the NHS has managed care during lockdown, speaks to experts and analyses new research, and also meets fellow patients to discover what the pandemic has meant for them.(n)

programme.title: Ceitidh Morag (Katie Morag)
programme.desc: Spring arrives, and the youngster is very fond of the new lambs.(n)

programme.title: Shane an Chef
programme.desc: Magaidh's hens escape, so Shane has no eggs to serve for his new breakfast menu.(n)

programme.title: Luthaidh (Louie)
programme.desc: When Yoko plans a tea party, Louie draws a pigeon to help them carry the invitations.(n)

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: The animals learn about the icicles, discovering they have a connection with water.(n)

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Anne Lorne Gillies reads Bogsa-dhèideagan Ciopair, written and illustrated by Mick Inkpen and translated into Gaelic by Jo MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Donnie Murdo (Danger Mouse)
programme.desc: Penfold is jealous of Danger Mouse's growing collection of medals, which leads him to fall under the spell of Quark's latest money-making scheme.(n)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to call room service on a home-made telephone, and the truth about the effects of baked beans. Kerry Anne MacLeod and Allan MacDonald present.(n)

programme.title: Aithne air Ainmhidhean (All About Animals)
programme.desc: In the shallows of Hawaii, life is hard. Catching enough food and avoiding bigger sharks isn't easy and only one in 10 baby hammerheads survives to see its first birthday. If she reaches the age of one Hula will have to leave Hawaii, otherwise she will starve. This means an epic journey to find waters teeming with life, thousands of kilometres away. Here, cameras observe as she learns ocean survival skills.(n)

programme.title: O Mo Dhùthaich (From Uist with Love)
programme.desc: Observational documentary following young shepherds Archie and Ben, and outdoors enthusiast and head gamekeeper Rory McGillivray as they enjoy North Uist Games day. Meanwhile, in South Uist, Angus and Neil are busy gathering seafood for export to Spain.(n)

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald.(n)

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)
programme.desc: Sports clubs, such as one concerned with bicycles and another synchronised swimming.(n)

programme.title: An Là (News)
programme.desc: Local, national and international news reports.(n)

programme.title: Ceathrar air a' Chuiltheann (Four on the Cuillin)
programme.desc: Vertigo, fatigue, hunger and the cold threatens the bid of school leavers Rosie, Zach, Mairead and Siobhan to tackle the Cuillin Ridge mountain climb.(n)

programme.title: Mathair
programme.desc: This programme tells the story of the 1981 Hunger Strike through the eyes of a mother, Alice McElwee, who had to suffer the death of her son.(n)

programme.title: Port
programme.desc: Music recorded in Edinburgh, Cork and Limerick in 2015 and 2016. Guests include Mairéad Ní Mhaonaigh, who joins Julie Fowlis, Phil Cunningham and Anna-Wendy Stevenson for a version of An Cailín Deas Óg.(n)

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Filmed during lockdown, Julie Fowlis invites musicians from around the world to perform from their own homes.(n)

programme.title: Eileanan na Banrighe/Islands of the Queen
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the diversity of the Commonwealth islands of the Queen. From weathered stone huts in the in upland moors to golden Caribbean beaches, the series shows the isles in all their contrast and glory.(n)

programme.title: Dhan Uisge
programme.desc: Calum Maclean explains his passion for wild swimming, and swims in the River Truim, which flows north in Badenoch into the Spey.(n)

programme.title: Air an Rathad (On the Road)
programme.desc: Donnie Mackay heads to the Grampian Transport Museum in Alford, for a car show with a difference. programme.title: Manhunt: The Raoul Moat Story
programme.desc: Ten years have passed since Newcastle bodybuilder Raoul Moat went on the run after shooting his ex-girlfriend Samantha Stobbart and her new boyfriend Chris Brown, killing the latter. Nicky Campbell presents this documentary looking back at the real-life drama as it unfolded in July 2010. Archive footage combine with interviews with family members, witnesses and journalists to piece together the tragic events that gripped the nation, and only ending when Moat shot himself dead while surrounded by armed police.(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: When the remains of a teenager are found half-buried in a wood, Vera finds herself investigating a 30-year-old mystery. Identified as Carrie Telling, a girl who disappeared during the 1984 miners' strike, the team retrieves news footage from the protests that shows her taking pictures in the background. It appears she got caught up in the unrest that day, contradicting her father's assertion that she went straight home. Photographs recovered from her camera yield further revelations for Vera. What did Carrie discover that ultimately led to her death?(n)

programme.title: European Cup Winners: Nottingham Forest
programme.desc: Between 1977 and 1984 English football teams dominated the European Cup and Nottingham Forest, under charismatic manager Brian Clough, lifted the famous trophy twice. Gabriel Clarke looks at how Clough and his assistant Peter Taylor built a championship-winning team and then shocked the world of football by dominating its premier club competition two years in a row. Featuring highlights of both of those finals and interviews with some of the key individuals involved.(n)

It appears she got caught up in the unrest that day, contradicting her father's assertion that she went straight home. Photographs recovered from her camera yield further revelations for Vera. What did Carrie discover that ultimately led to her death?(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

Paul adamantly denies having even met the woman making the allegation and his loyal wife (Julie Walters) must decide whether to stand by him, while his troubled daughter (Andrea Riseborough) begins to question her relationship with her father.(n)

programme.title: The Man with the 10-Stone Testicles
programme.desc: The extraordinary story of Las Vegas man Wesley Warren Jr, whose testicles started swelling following an accident in 2008, meaning he could no longer live a normal life. The rare condition, referred to by doctors as scrotal elephantiasis or scrotal lymphedema, was caused by excess watery fluid collecting in tissues of the body, and the film follows Wesley before and after surgery on his giant scrotum.(n)

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: A 19-year-old computer enthusiast goes on the first date of his life, while a music producer and a singer who both enjoyed pop success in the 1970s meet at Fred's restaurant. Other guests in this episode include a PR executive who always finds one little thing that puts her off every man she meets, and two strangers with a love of dancing.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Sushi Ko, a Japanese restaurant in California that is losing up to $20,000 a month. Gordon finds owner and chef Akira lacking in confidence and self-esteem as the strain of an empty business is beginning to take its toll on his relationship with this family.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Newcastle is the location as Rosalind and Roy present an evening of refined dining, Paul and Vanessa focus on their entertainment and Skev and Kayleigh serve up an eclectic menu.(n)

programme.title: Flirty Dancing
programme.desc: Ashley Banjo tries to bring singletons together through the art of dance, teaching strangers one half of a fully choreographed routine, and only bringing them together just once on the day that they dance together. But there is no speaking allowed and afterwards they must part silently, deciding separately whether they want to see each other again. Up first are senior events manager Hannah and e-commerce manager James, and graphic designer Luke and civil servant Dan. Will sparks fly on the dance floor?(n)

Fact-based drama, starring Jonny Weston, Gerard Butler and Elisabeth Shue.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Michael Buble impersonator Jamie Somers hosts the final Merseyside dinner party, but may have to put aside his differences with guest Jade if he is to be crowned the winner of the £1,000 cash prize.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.desc: Amy finds she has romantic feelings for Penny's former boyfriend Zack, while Raj and Howard begin competing to prove which of them would make the better superhero. Comedy, guest starring Brian Thomas Smith.(n)

programme.title: The Battle of Britain: 3 Days That Saved the Nation
programme.desc: Dan Snow and Kate Humble conclude their guide to aerial battle that changed the course of the Second World War. Here they tell the story of September 15, 1940 - the most decisive day in the battle for the RAF. With Hitler's plan to invade just days away, Britain's pilots had a final chance to save the nation against the Luftwaffe.(n)

programme.title: The Royals in Wartime

programme.title: Nazi Megastructures

This programme explores the story behind one of the most technologically advanced aeroplanes of the Second World War, awe-inspiring fighter jet the Messerschmitt Me 262, and the huge subterranean cave where it was built.(n)

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Jon Bentley gets his hands on three of the latest soundbars, which promise to bring high-end cinematic audio to any self-respecting living room. Georgie Barrat heads into the rugged mountains of north Wales to take a ride on the world's longest zip line, while testing out a robust new drone ready to take to the skies - even in the wind and rain. The G Team jumps in at the deep end with three gadgets aimed at helping an aspiring swimmer take vital seconds off a lap time. Plus, Craig Charles tries to set up a wireless security system.(n)

programme.title: Great Coastal Railway Journeys
programme.desc: A train ride along the most attractive coastlines in Europe. From Spain's northern seas to the beautiful Welsh coast and the frozen wonders of Norway, this documentary charts how each coast was shaped and linked by rail and meets some of the characters who live and work along these most scenic lines.(n)

programme.title: A Greek Odyssey with Bettany Hughes
programme.desc: The historian arrives in the Peloponnese, a peninsula regarded as home to some of ancient Greece's most legendary kings and vicious warriors. To understand the violent world in which the myths and legends are set, Bettany visits the bones of a 19-year-old-warrior who died more than 3,500 years ago. Healed sword marks and a large hole in his skull are a testament to his environment. In Sparta, the home town of Helen of Troy, Bettany discovers what made the region's warriors so feared, before heading to Mycenae, likely home to the kings and queens that inspired the story of the Trojan War.(n)

programme.title: Shirley Valentine (1989)
programme.desc: A Liverpudlian housewife is trapped in a humdrum existence and a dull marriage. Fed up, she decides to go on an unheard-of holiday by herself to a Greek island, where she starts to enjoy life again - especially when she is romanced and seduced by a smooth-talking fisherman. Comedy, adapted from Willy Russell's stage play, starring Pauline Collins, Tom Conti, Bernard Hill, Julia McKenzie and Alison Steadman.(n)

Six-week-old golden retriever Dakin demonstrates how his ever-developing brain helps improve his stature and coordination, and a litter of bull mastiff pups are left baffled by a mirror. programme.desc: Highlights of the singer's first river cruise, which was along the Danube on board the SS Maria Theresa, starting in Budapest and taking in Vienna before finishing in Passau in southern Bavaria, Then, Jane heads off on a Portuguese deluxe trip down the river Douro, visiting Lamego and Vila Real and ending in the capital Lisbon.(n)

programme.title: The Queen: Duty Before Family?
programme.desc: Documentary examining how the Queen has dealt with family crises during her near-70 year reign, from denying her sister Margaret permission to marry Peter Townsend, to the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, and the fallout from Prince Andrew's friendship with Jeffrey Epstein. Insiders reveal the story of how she has faced those difficult decisions and how there can only ever one winner when it comes to duty and family.(n)

programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong
programme.desc: A compilation of television mishaps, including the time a fire alarm on Channel 4 News left presenter Krishnan Guru-Murthy making the headlines rather than reporting them. Anthea Turner reflects on the horror of being set on fire in front of an audience of millions, comedian Bobby Davro opens up about his show when the laughter turned into horror, and journalist Michael Crick looks back at a cringeworthy encounter with an angry MP.(n)

programme.title: Distant Drums (1951)
programme.desc: An army officer mounts a daring raid through the treacherous Florida swamplands. His mission is to take on a group of marauding Seminole rebels, as well as the gun runners who have been supplying them with weapons, and rescue a group of settlers who have been taken hostage by the tribe. Western, starring Gary Cooper, Mari Aldon, Richard Webb, Ray Teal and Arthur Hunnicutt.(n)

programme.title: Moonrise (1948)
programme.desc: A man is haunted by the fact that his late father was a murderer, something no-one will ever let him forget. His problems are further compounded when he accidentally kills a man in self-defence - and realising people will think it was malicious, he decides to hide the body. Drama, starring Dane Clark, Gail Russell and Ethel Barrymore.(n)

programme.title: Alvarez Kelly (1966)
programme.desc: A cynical, mercenary cattle drover sets out to deliver a herd to soldiers at a Union base during the American Civil War. During the journey, he is kidnapped by a Confederate colonel, who hopes to persuade him to help steal the animals and take them deep into rebel territory. Western, starring William Holden, Richard Widmark and Janice Rule.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Teacher (1959)
programme.desc: A headmaster hopes to land a more prestigious job at a better school, but his pupils do not want him to leave. When two inspectors pay a visit, the mischievous students see a chance to sabotage his career plans with a series of pranks. Comedy, starring Ted Ray, Kenneth Connor, Charles Hawtrey and Kenneth Williams.(n)

programme.title: Back to the Future Part III (1990)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly is transported back to the days of the Wild West, where he strives to save his eccentric scientist friend Doc Brown from death at the hands of a villainous gunslinger. Final instalment in the sci-fi comedy trilogy, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Mary Steenburgen and Thomas F Wilson.(n)

programme.title: Independence Day: Resurgence (2016)
programme.desc: Two decades have passed since Earth was saved from an extraterrestrial invasion, and in the years since, the aliens' salvaged technology has been used to transform the world. However, it may not be enough to save the human race when a second, even deadlier attack begins. Sci-fi adventure, starring Jeff Goldblum, Liam Hemsworth and Bill Pullman.(n)

programme.title: Dawn of the Planet of the Apes (2014)
programme.desc: A colony of super-intelligent apes thrives following a global virus outbreak that wipes out much of the human race. A survivor tries to make peace with the leader of the apes, but their efforts to reach an understanding are threatened by warmongers on both sides. Sci-fi thriller sequel, starring Andy Serkis, Jason Clarke and Gary Oldman.(n)

programme.title: She, A Chinese (2009)
programme.desc: A restless woman leaves her dull life in a small Chinese village in search of excitement, but ends getting drawn into organised crime. When her gangster lover is killed, she flees China for England, where she enters into a marriage of convenience. Drama, starring Huang Lu and We Yi Bo. In Mandarin and English.(n)

programme.title: Hell Is for Heroes (1962)
programme.desc: A rebellious soldier defies orders to lead two men on a secret attack on a German pillbox, but his mission fails and the other two soldiers are killed. Court-martialled for his actions, he remains convinced his plan was sound and ignores the sentence to make a second attempt at destroying the emplacement. Second World War adventure, with Steve McQueen, James Coburn and Bobby Darin.(n)

programme.title: The Fighting Kentuckian (1949)
programme.desc: French settlers are granted asylum in America in the aftermath of Napoleon's defeat at Waterloo and a Kentucky rifleman finds himself falling for one of the newcomers - a general's daughter. However, he faces a rival for her affections in the form of a ruthless landowner who plans to steal the terrritory her fellow exiles have settled on. George Waggner's Western, starring John Wayne, Vera Hruba Ralston, Philip Dorn and Oliver Hardy.(n)

programme.title: Funeral in Berlin (1966)
programme.desc: Former crook and disreputable spy Harry Palmer has to carefully engineer the defection of a Russian officer responsible for safeguarding vital military secrets - but the mission proves less than straightforward. Cold War thriller and the second film to feature Len Deighton's character, starring Michael Caine, Eva Renzi, Oscar Homolka, Paul Hubschmid and Guy Doleman.(n)

programme.title: Gun Fury (1953)
programme.desc: A wealthy rancher and his fiancee take the stagecoach to California, where they intend to start a new life in the wake of the Civil War. However, during the journey an ex-Confederate outlaw and his gang of thugs attack the coach and kidnap the bride-to-be. Distraught and wounded, the rancher sets out to rescue her and punish her abductors. Western, with Rock Hudson, Donna Reed, Lee Marvin and Phil Carey.(n)

programme.title: Ender's Game (2013)
programme.desc: In the future, the human race has survived conflict with an alien race, but the prospect of war breaking out again is a constant threat. A gifted boy is selected as a potential military commander for the coming conflict, and subjected to a gruelling training program to prove his worth. Sci-fi adventure based on Orson Scott Card's novel, starring Asa Butterfield, Harrison Ford and Ben Kingsley.(n)

programme.title: War for the Planet of the Apes (2017)
programme.desc: A devastating conflict erupts between humans and intelligent primates in a post-apocalyptic future. The leader of the apes is determined to make peace and prove his people are not savages - but a tragic course of events leaves him wrestling with a desire for revenge. Sci-fi adventure sequel, the conclusion of the trilogy of prequels to the 1968 movie. Starring Andy Serkis, Woody Harrelson and Steve Zahn.(n)

programme.title: Escape Plan (2013)
programme.desc: An expert takes a contract from the CIA to test a maximum security prison by trying to break out of it, but he soon discovers he has been duped and that his captors intend him to stay inside for ever. He hatches a plan to escape, but to succeed he will need the help of a long-term convict - who wants his own freedom in return for assistance. Thriller, with Sylvester Stallone and Arnold Schwarzenegger.(n)

programme.title: Juan of the Dead (2011)
programme.desc: Cuba is overrun by brain-eating zombies, which the government tries to cover up as the work of American-backed dissidents. A 40-year-old slacker realises what is going on, and hatches a scheme to go into business as a zombie slayer, laying the dead to rest for a price. Comedy horror, starring Alexis Diaz de Villegas and Jorge Molina. In Spanish.(n)
