programme.desc: When Adam creates a video that makes it look as though he and his friends beat Barry in a game of basketball, Barry demands a rematch in the school gym. Beverly tries to throw a 'surprise' party for Murray's 50th birthday, despite his objections to the idea.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard faces the difficult decision of choosing between his relationship with Priya and his friendship with Penny, while Sheldon is irritated by one of Howard's magic tricks. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is distraught when his online gaming account is hacked, so his friends decide to find out who is responsible. However, they make the disastrous mistake of confronting the culprit in person. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy and Sheldon start a gossip experiment to see how quickly rumours spread among the gang, while Howard tells his friends he is planning to propose to Bernadette - not realising Raj is wrestling with his own feelings for her. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Mayim Bialik and Johnny Galecki.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is infuriated when Priya uses her legal knowledge to find a loophole in the room-mate agreement - but his spirits are lifted by a night on the town with Amy, Penny and Bernadette. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy and Penny use Bernadette to spy on Leonard and Priya after the couple invite her and Howard to dinner. An experimental drug helps Raj overcome his fear of talking to women, but proves more effective than he had expected. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard decides to tell his mother that he and Bernadette are engaged - but she later collapses, making him fear the shock of the news has caused her to suffer a heart attack. As the gang convenes at the hospital, Sheldon's fear of infectious diseases lands him in trouble, while Priya and Penny bond over coffee.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Bernadette receives her PhD, and the friends then tease Howard about being the only one among them who is not a doctor. Meanwhile, Raj becomes Sheldon's new flatmate after overhearing Leonard and Priya engaging in a Star Trek bedroom fantasy. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kunal Nayyar.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny worries about the consequences of spending the night with Raj, and Leonard's reaction confirms her worst fears. Meanwhile, Sheldon is more interested in the upcoming paintball competition, and tries to refocus the boys' attentions as they argue over the previous evening's activities. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys invite the girls to a Dungeons and Dragons night when their trip to Vegas falls through after an incident with airport security. However, following a few drinks, Sheldon and Amy's relationship takes an unexpected turn. Meanwhile, Raj goes on a date with Lucy, who tries to sneak out of the restaurant's bathroom window - but ends up getting stuck and calling for his help.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is offered an exciting job opportunity, but it will mean he has to leave the country for several months. Penny tries to support his ambitions, while Sheldon is jealous and attempts to discourage him from accepting the post. Meanwhile, Raja pushes the boundaries with Lucy, but puts their relationship in jeopardy.(n)

programme.title: The Hunger Games: Catching Fire (2013)
programme.desc: Katniss Everdeen's victory in a televised death match makes her a focus for revolution against the totalitarian nation she lives in. The rulers of the regime plot to crush dissent with a new series of games, in which past champions battle it out. Sci-fi adventure sequel based on the second book in Suzanne Collins' trilogy of novels, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Liam Hemsworth, Donald Sutherland and Philip Seymour Hoffman.(n)

programme.title: Bad Neighbours 2 (2016)
programme.desc: A couple who have finally rid themselves of the college fraternity next door become stuck with even worse new neighbours in the shape of a raucous college sorority. In desperation, they turn to their old enemies in getting rid of the new arrivals. Comedy, starring Seth Rogen, Zac Efron, Rose Byrne and Chloe Grace Moretz.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox USA
programme.desc: Sharon and Kelly Osbourne, Tyra Banks and Meghan Trainor cast an eye over Jersey Shore: Family Vacation, Hostile Planet, Magic for Humans, and The Shawshank Redemption.(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: What do Britain's sharpest armchair critics think of Saturday Night Takeaway, Celebrity Haunted Mansion, Point Break, Britain's Brightest Family, Portrait Artist of the Year, Troy: Fall of a City and the News? From March 2018.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp looks back to the 1970s for vintage inspiration in this bite-sized episode. The presenter creates crocheted jellyfish and a colourful felt rug with the help of two of Britain's most gifted craftspeople.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Sylvester and Anthonia Swanston from West Sussex in their search for a holiday home in the Davenport area of Florida, showing them five properties for their £300,000 budget. But will any meet the couple's exacting standards?(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole tries to restore a monkey bike, and Simon O'Brien makes use of all his skills as he tries to give new life to an old racing bicycle.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien finds a barn containing a collection of replica Rolls-Royce cars, and Henry Cole tries his hand at driving a tractor as they continue their money-making tour.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: A look back at the transformation of Dick Strawbridge and his wife Angel's 45-room chateau from an abandoned ruin into a sumptuous family home. This episode looks back at how the abandoned walled garden stole Dick and Angel's hearts and how the family members fell for the country lifestyle, with their children growing up learning about seasons, livestock and wildlife.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Gavin Panella and Chantal Schaul are self-building a little bit of continental Europe in the heart of Norfolk. Chantal's from Luxembourg, where self-building is much more common. They've opted for a fixed-price kit, but also want to add plenty of personal touches, so the costs are racking up. What is more, they're building in a challenging plot, which calls for some imaginative design features. It's going to be one of the most colourful homes that Charlie Luxton has ever seen and he has lots of suggestions to help them realise their bright ideas.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Alston House in Cumbria, where owners Michael and Carole Allchorne are brimming with confidence, but Yvonne Copeland and Gail Wells are less than impressed with the decor. The guests are impressed with chef Michael's breakfast, but the hosts are left reeling by the criticisms levelled at their hotel during the feedback session.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Scotia House in Harrogate, where host Marie Lock is hoping her obsessive cleaning regime will give her the edge in the competition. In the rooms, both Michael and Carole and Yvonne and Gail find the deluxe teddy bears freaky, and for Billy and Billy, things are a little on the cosy side. In the afternoon, Marie's keen to show off her moves and pits her guests against each other in a Northern Soul dance off.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Canberra Guest House in Blackpool for the third day of the competition, where host Yvonne Copeland, joined by her part-time helper Gail Wells, hopes her cheap and cheerful B&B will win over her guests. However, Marie finds what she thinks is urine and hair on the toilet seat in her room, and a lack of wi-fi means Billy Junior has to put his business on hold. Yvonne takes her competitors to a local reptile house, but serious divisions unfold within the group, and later, tensions rise again at dinner.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Crown and Kitchen in East Linton, East Lothian, owned by father and son Billy and Billy Jr Robertson, who see themselves as the jokers of the pack. Yvonne and Gail leave no mattress unturned in their room inspection, although Michael and Carole are impressed. Later, Yvonne makes quite a splash when the hosts organise an assault course for their guests, but slow service during breakfast leaves the B&Bers disappointed - especially when Billy Jr is at a loss to explain what the problem was.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Things are tense from the start as Carole and Michael from Alston House challenge Marie on her comments. Next, Marie from Scotia House wants to set the record straight with allegations of tit-for-tat scoring. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Drive It
programme.desc: Henry Cole is trying to set a new vintage land speed record on his supercharged classic Triumph, but needs classic race transportation to help the dream become a reality. So, he and Sam Lovegrove buy a 1968 Morris ambulance to restore and transform into the quintessential vintage race transporter.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Kevin and Natalie discover their beloved six-year-old German shepherd has a tumour. In desperation, they turn to Noel Fitzpatrick in the hope he can find a way to prolong Otto's life until their wedding day. The team also sees Jane, Simon and their Rottweiler puppy Elvis, who is having trouble walking, and seven-week-old Pomeranian Gucci, brought in with a broken paw by Amber and her two children after only 48 hours in her new home.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree help British people making French castles their home or running businesses from them. In the opening episode, ther duo give Johnny a crash course in the dos and don'ts of chateau shopping before he and his wife start the search for their own dream place. Jonathan and Michael, who are struggling to turn their magnificent French castle into a money-making venture, explore some of their chateau's caves in the hope of finding a buried fortune. Plus, Phil and Angelina struggle to pitch their chateau as the perfect place for a 50th wedding anniversary party when they can't see eye to eye themselves.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to Sussex to see how Ben Law's woodsman's cottage has changed. The house was built on a low budget, using methods dating back to the early medieval period with raw materials from the surrounding countryside. During the past 18 months the upstairs has been completed and the outside has been landscaped. Kevin also discovers how the property has changed Ben's life since the original programme was broadcast.(n)

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: Jamie and Claire keep secrets from one another as they try to help Brianna process her recent trauma. But they only served to cause a bigger family rift once they are revealed.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy game show, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson joined by guests Kathy Burke and Kevin Bridges. Susie Dent is assisted in Dictionary Corner by Joe Lycett, while Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers with a little help from Nick Helm.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Bishop Brennan descends on Craggy Island to investigate rumours that Father Jack has developed the unfortunate habit of sleepwalking in the nude - only to come face to face with his worst fear. Clerical comedy, with Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The cleric tries to nurture Chris, the hot favourite to win the King of the Sheep festival, back to top form after he is left shaken by a terrifying encounter. Meanwhile, Dougal becomes convinced Craggy Island is being terrorised by a monster.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 78-year-old man has difficulty breathing after breaking several ribs by tripping over a kerb, and doctors discover that a woman is in the latter stages of pancreatic cancer when they treat her for sepsis. Elsewhere, a 32-year-old woman is treated after bruising her neck and face when she slipped on wet decking on a night out with friends.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for the Grasshopper Also in Carlstadt, New Jersey, a family-owned Irish restaurant. There, he lends a helping hand to Mitch Sandler, who has been struggling to live up to the expectations of his father-in-law since he invested heavily in the business. The chef also tries to improve the presentation of dishes emerging from the kitchen.(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy game show, with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson joined by guests Kathy Burke and Kevin Bridges. Susie Dent is assisted in Dictionary Corner by Joe Lycett, while Rachel Riley provides the letters and numbers with a little help from Nick Helm.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal and Ted get involved in yet another bout of rivalry with their counterparts over on Rugged Island - this time by writing a song that will allow them the dubious honour of representing Ireland in the Eurovision-style music contest Eurosong. Ardal O'Hanlon and Dermot Morgan star.(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home.(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp joins jewellery-maker Rachel Jeffrey to help a soon-to-be-married couple create their own wedding rings, and learns how gold can revive an old picture frame at little cost.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Bedfordshire couple Dean and Karen Dhinsa find a rural retreat in southern Tenerife. programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien arrive in York, where Henry struggles to squeeze into some motorcycle leathers and Simon gets into deep water when he tries to restore an aluminium boat.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a stately home in Somerset, where Simon is convinced he has uncovered a genuine Tudor relic.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree help British people making French castles their home or running businesses from them. In the opening episode, ther duo give Johnny a crash course in the dos and don'ts of chateau shopping before he and his wife start the search for their own dream place. Jonathan and Michael, who are struggling to turn their magnificent French castle into a money-making venture, explore some of their chateau's caves in the hope of finding a buried fortune. Plus, Phil and Angelina struggle to pitch their chateau as the perfect place for a 50th wedding anniversary party when they can't see eye to eye themselves.(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton heads to Herefordshire to meet couple Tim and Fliss, who have been fighting to get planning permission for their new home for three years while living in a static caravan, and hope to save £70,000 through a self-built, timber-framed house when they get the go-ahead to start work on a small plot of family land.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Newton House in Knaresborough, North Yorkshire, where Denise Carter and Jackie Fennell's traditional B&B focuses on organic, free-range breakfasts. Over dinner, guest Marianne Cammack and Deina Thomas from dominate the conversation, riling up some of the others, while Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson think the morning meal is all way too fancy.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to the Highlands Pub in Blackpool, where managers Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson are all about value for money. However, Sean and Kathryn are horrified by what they find in their room, while Marianne and Deina think that theirs needs the female touch. In the afternoon the group competes in a basketball challenge at the amusement arcades, where the quiet hosts come out of their shells.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the White House in Brighton for the third day of the competition, where hosts Sean Edlin and Kathryn Turnbull run a laid-back seaside B&B. Denise and Jackie think the rooms are not well equipped and Marianne finds them clinical, while Mike and Neal are unsure about the price. Tensions are temporarily alleviated by a visit to the famous Theatre Royal, but Marianne and Deina continue to cause trouble at dinner. In the morning, a locally sourced breakfast impresses some of the guests, but the group must decide whether they are getting value for money.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wakes Hall Lodges near Colchester is the fourth and final visit of the week, where Neal and Mike are wowed by the five-star self-catering accommodation, complete with hot tubs and lakeside views. The others aren't so sure about the 'luxury' breakfast hamper, however, especially as it means cooking their own. An afternoon of dog agility gives manager Marianne Cammack the chance to demonstrate her authority, although over dinner, her domineering conversation causes Jackie to finally snap.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Mike and Neal from the Highlands Pub challenge the comments that they were considered dirty and lacking in facilities, while Sean and Kathryn from the White House are left smarting after suffering large underpayments for what they think are minor issues. Eventually, the winner is finally revealed.(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Drive It
programme.desc: Car restorers Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove hunt for a slice of the American Dream - a classic American pickup truck that Henry wants to use as a daily driver. Henry also explores the history of the pick-up wagon and takes a drive in an original Model T Ford, before restoring his own truck with a custom 'rat rod' finish.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Cavachon Stampy has been hit by a car and is in a critical condition, leaving owners Jim and his wife Karen facing a difficult dilemma - to take the chance and put their pet through painful surgery, or let him die. Golden retriever Dylan has a limp, and owner Stuart has driven seven hours from Preston to seek Noel Fitzpatrick's help. Meanwhile, Ginny from Chelsea arrives with her beloved dachshund Lilly-Belle, who has lost the use of her back legs.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Angel Adoree advises Jonathan and Michael on how to make their chateau wedding business work. Then it's crunch time for the pair as they try to persuade an American couple to book a big-money wedding with them. Meanwhile, Phil and Angelina clear their garden for a wedding anniversary party, but struggle to dispose of the debris. Elsewhere, Johnny and Ashley view two properties as they search for a dream chateau.(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Tim and Zoe Bawtree, who live in a costly Regency property in Cheltenham, Gloucestershire, plan to build a modern, low-maintenance house in their back garden. However, height restrictions mean 60 per cent of their new home has to be underground and there are 90 objections from neighbours to their planning application. Undeterred, the pair press ahead, only to encounter further problems - including the departure of the builders.(n)

programme.title: Code 37: Sex Crimes
programme.desc: Kevin and Hannah are summoned when Lisa, a patient in a psychiatric institution is raped while sleeping. Hannah is convinced that the rapist is within the institution and worries about a recurrence if the team fails to act quickly enough. Belgian crime thriller, starring Veerle Baetens and Gilles De Schryver. In Flemish.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors suspect a blood clot when David, 69, is rushed in with severe leg pain and a pale foot, and vascular specialists are called to assess him for surgery to save his leg. Annie, 90, has been brought in after struggling to breathe at home. Her daughter Francesca and granddaughter Tamara wait by her side in resus, as doctors battle to uncover the cause of shadowing on her lungs.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Patients whose visions of the world are rocked by life-changing events, including 44-year-old Sergio, who is flown in by air ambulance after a cycling accident. Another cyclist - 71-year-old Alan - arrives in resus after being in a collision with a van. Doctors suspect a head injury and multiple fractures. Alan's wife Jenny reveals how she met him and how a sudden accident involving her son burst their idyll and took their lives on an unexpected path.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors suspect a blood clot when David, 69, is rushed in with severe leg pain and a pale foot, and vascular specialists are called to assess him for surgery to save his leg. Annie, 90, has been brought in after struggling to breathe at home. programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty wants to know why jelly won't set if chopped papaya is added to it, so he visits South Africa and discovers that the exotic fruit contains enzymes that can also help tenderise meat. In Hawaii, Kate Quilton visits America's only tuna auction to find out why some fish have dangerous levels of mercury, while others don't. She also visits a groundbreaking tech start-up in San Francisco that hopes to feed the world by growing fish flesh in a dish. Matt Tebbutt finds out why homemade ice cubes are cloudy, when the ones bought in shops are clear.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman heads to northern Tenerife to help Edinburgh couple John Clark and David Cameron find a dwelling that will eventually become their retirement home. With their wedding just weeks away, the pair have £100,000 to find somewhere that will accommodate a future mother-in-law as well as five visiting children.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Bedfordshire couple Liz and Grahame Pink, who have saved hard for their dream holiday home in Lanzarote and have a budget of £63,000 to spend on a property.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Emma Greenfield and Daniel Halley search for a home on the Canary Island of Fuerteventura with a budget of £73,000. Presenter Jasmine Harman is on hand to show them a selection of properties on the market.(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps Jay and Charlene, parents to one-year-old Kai, who bought and refurbished their two bed home in Romford, Essex but now want to upsize to Chelmsford. Meanwhile, Phil Spencer assists Jemma and Graham, who returned from Los Angeles three years ago and want to settle in Southend-on-Sea.(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Liam Charles and Tom Allen welcome back the three best teams from both sets of heats, who join forces to face new rivals and their toughest challenges so far. For the first round, the teams are asked to create 24 perfectly identical classic kouign-amann and soaked babas in just four hours. In the second challenge, they must make a sugar showpiece inspired by insects incorporating 30 lollipops and 30 jelly sweets. Benoit Blin and Cherish Finden have to choose five of the teams to go through to the quarter finals.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Five people from Swindon take turns trying to throw the perfect dinner party. International DJ Mark Eteson hosts the first night and hopes his Ibiza-style menu featuring Bora Bora Beach brownies, paella and fishcakes will be a hit, before challenging his guests to a rave-off which he will judge himself.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Amateur dramatics enthusiast Michaela Kearns hosts the second party in Swindon, but admits she is not much of a cook. She hopes to dazzle her guests with a glamour-filled evening featuring melted chocolate with dipping treats, but anticipates she might not be able to persuade her fellow diners to take part in her planned show tune performance - complete with a full band.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Financial advisor Scott Hillier hosts Swindon's third party, hoping to impress his guests with a Wild West-themed evening. Tensions run high as Gill Bright awkwardly apologises to last night's host Michaela Kearns and Scott Hillier hopes to smooth things over by performing a rap.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Direct sales manager Sally Rachael hosts the fourth dinner party in Swindon, featuring a 'girls' night in' theme with mysteriously titled dishes and a quirky dress code that leaves her guests clueless and unhappy.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Alternative therapist Gill Bright hosts the final dinner party in Swindon and has plans for an 'upstairs downstairs' Edwardian-themed evening with sophisticated food followed by some circle dancing to Bob Marley.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Vale of Berkeley Guest House in Gloucestershire, where owners Harri and Claire Chiba hope to prove that their establishment offers the best value for money. However, two guests from rival B&Bs have an issue with the no-frills toilet, and another places a tricky request for 'anaemic toast' at the breakfast table.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Artist Residence in Penzance, Cornwall, where proprietors Justin and Charlie hope to impress their rivals with a pottery class. However, one pair decide their room is too modern for their tastes, a duo are shocked to find hair in a shower filter and one guest seems underwhelmed by breakfast.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Third to host are Alec and Craig Coleman, who open the doors to the Edenfield Guest House in Blackpool and invite their fellow B&B owners to rate their establishment. However, on arrival one of the guests and his wife discover stains on the bedspread, while others feel the 'award-winning' food is not up to scratch.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the New Inn, Portland, Dorset, for the fourth day of the competition, where hosts Stacey and Spencer Cullum introduce their guests to a typical island activity - sandcastle building. Meanwhile, Claire Chiba quizzes Stacey about her breakfast hamper and says she is alienating customers, before Harri Chiba tells Justin that his B&B is too 'manufactured'.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners meet one last time to discover how much their rivals thought they should receive and to settle some scores. Two contestants attempt to explain away their negative feedback, and one contender finds herself unable to hold back her frustration, before the winner of the competition is revealed.(n)

programme.title: The Queen's Lost Family
programme.desc: The inside story of the royal family over three turbulent decades from the 1920s to the end of the Second World War. It begins just after the First World War, as monarchies throughout Europe have toppled, and most working men and many women now have the vote.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow patients at south west London's St George's hospital on royal wedding day. As Harry and Meghan tie the knot, patients and their relatives reflect on the meaning of lifelong love, devotion and commitment. Among those treated are David, 74, who is flown to St George's by air ambulance after falling from a ladder. Medics suspect internal bleeding and he is rushed to CT to assess the damage. His wife Lynda makes her way to the hospital as doctors try to prevent catastrophic blood loss.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cameras follow Aydigul, 38, who has suspected sepsis following major abdominal surgery. Her sister is at her bedside throughout, and discusses their childhood in Tajikistan and her extraordinary strength in the face of adversity. Plus, emergency consultant Ahmed rushes to save the life of a woman who has an abdominal aortic aneurysm.(n)

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.desc: Dougal and Ted visit the mainland, where they bump into actor Richard Wilson - and discover the hard way that he does not take kindly to being confused with his comic alter ego Victor Meldrew. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon star.(n)

programme.desc: Kevin and Andrew Buckles resolve to get fit, but with no gyms available in the backwoods, the lads assemble their own rustic fitness gear.(n)

programme.desc: DIY masters Kevin and Andrew Buckles try to transform their quaint family retreat into a casino.(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends

Rick learns the essential etiquette to the Austrian capital's coffee house culture and indulges in its sweet tooth by enjoying a plate or two of apple strudel and Sacher torte. He also finds time to enjoy the traditional music of the country, typically Mozart and Strauss.(n)

programme.title: Top Gear

programme.title: World War Weird

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Walmer castle, Kent, and is challenged to identify a valuable 18th-century glass out of a collection of reproductions. Other items brought in to be valued include a rare pottery piglet, a revolving table with secret compartments, a British PoW's diary, a set of French fashion illustrations from the 1920s, a table clock and a collection of Victorian ceramic birds.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Brighton as he starts the final leg of his journey from Norwich to Chichester, learning about the history of the Royal Pavilion. At Bramber, he discovers that Victorian tourists flocked to the town in huge numbers to visit a museum of taxidermy, before heading into the South Downs for a taste of life in the fast lane at Goodwood.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels the length and breadth of the country with a copy of George Bradshaw's Victorian Railway Guidebook to discover how the railways have affected people and find out what remains of Bradshaw's Britain. He begins in Liverpool by learning to speak Scouse, before researching the first railway fatality and exploring the origins of the Eccles cake.(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the second of two programmes from Walmer Castle in Kent, where the team values a jacket worn by President Kennedy that could be one of the most valuable items ever featured on the series. Other items brought in include an art deco mask that terrifies its owner's children, an enamel advertising sign, and a letter from Lord Nelson that has an unexpected connection to the owner's dead husband.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: Naturalist David Attenborough explores animal oddities, looking at two different animals with curiously distinctive evolutionary quirks, linked together by a common theme. In this instalment, he examines how hedgehogs and rhinos have developed protective skins.(n)

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines the vision in squid and owls, which have both developed incredible adaptations to help them navigate at night.(n)

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built

programme.title: WW2 - Battles for Europe
programme.desc: Eye-witness veteran testimony from men who served under fire on the frontline, taking viewers to the heart of iconic moments in the history of warfare. The first episode looks at the Allied invasion of Europe, which began on June 6 1944 - D-Day. British, American and Canadian troops stormed the beaches of Normandy in what would become known as The Longest Day.(n)

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The people responsible for tracking down war criminals who had evaded capture, beginning with the US army task force in charge of locating German scientists.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The second leg of Michael Portillo's journey from Liverpool to Scarborough takes him from Manchester to Bury. Along the way, he finds out about the life of Victorian train enthusiast George Bradshaw, gets fitted for a trilby hat in Denton, and learns how railways helped contribute to the nation's love affair with fish and chips.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey, travelling on the Embsay and Bolton Abbey steam railway, finding out about Roman discoveries in York, and taking to the air in the Network Rail helicopter.(n)

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Train Truckers

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The story of Hitler's personal secretary Martin Bormann and the efforts to track him down. With insufficient evidence to confirm the Nazi official's death, the International Military Tribunal at Nuremberg tried him in absentia during 1946 and eventually sentenced him to death. Despite extensive efforts, he was never found and was eventually presumed to have died in May 1945.(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey describes how Winston Churchill drew on the lessons of his ancestor John to lead the UK through the Second World War. At two key moments, the prime minister's strategic sense helped avert disaster, and in both decisions, the influence of the Duke of Marlborough is apparent. Starkey argues that it was Winston's ability to control events by giving them a narrative meaning that enabled him to shape world history long after his fall from power.(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on a 45-mile walk across Wiltshire, from Avebury to Stonehenge, telling the story of the remarkable development that occurred in the latter days of the Neolithic era. programme.desc: The chef travels to Flamangos restaurant in Whitehouse, New Jersey, where he tries to help the mother-and-daughter owners turn around their struggling business's fortunes. He faces a struggle because parent Adelle has a bad relationship with her staff and is disappointed by the resident chef, leaving Gordon with the added challenge of changing her attitude.(n)

programme.title: Jimmy's Forest
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty charts the seasonal transformation of a forest in Norfolk over the course of a year, beginning in spring. He undertakes experiments to learn more about the woodland using a well-equipped tree house with its own science lab as his base, preparing a meal using roadkill, getting to grips with a woodpecker and learning what blue tits and butter-icing have in common.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Denise Van Outen and her partner Eddie, Mo Gilligan and Babatunde Aleshe, Nick Grimshaw and his niece Liv, Joe Swash and Stacey Solomon, Zoe Ball and her son Woody Cook, Martin Kemp and son Roman Kemp, Maureen Lipman and Gyles Brandreth, Johnny Vegas and his PA Bev, Shaun Ryder and Bez, Emilia Fox and Joanna David, Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford, Laura Whitmore and Iain Stirling, Rylan Clark-Neal and Linda Clark, Georgia Taylor and Sunetra Sarker, and Susanna Reid and Rob Rinder offer sharp, insightful, funny and sometimes emotional critiques. Shows under scrutiny include 127 Hours, Planet Earth II, Cape Fear, This Morning on public toilets, EastEnders, Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad, and BBC news reports on beaches and travel.(n)

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Dating show hosted by Anna Richardson in which two singletons choose a date from a selection of six naked people, who are revealed to them one body part at a time. This time, Judith, a 57-year-old breast cancer survivor from Nuneaton, and Rigby, a 27-year-old hot tub installer from Chesterfield, take part.(n)

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke delves into the world of small builds, meeting people taking tiny, unpromising spaces and creating places to live, work and play. In the fourth episode, George is amazed by a couple's plan to turn a coach into a mobile home, complete with a garage for their Mini. He also meets a pilot who has created a garden den from a small plane. Elsewhere, George's observatory build takes him to one of the world's largest telescopes.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A look back at some of Noel Fitzpatrick's most memorable cases, focusing on some of the extraordinary customised implants and surgeries Noel has performed to save his patients. The documentary updates the stories of rescue cat Peanut, who had severely deformed front legs, retriever puppy Murphy, who sustained a shocking injury in a dog attack that ripped apart his lower jaw, and Alaskan malamute Adam, who needed customised knee-replacement implants if he was to ever walk again.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 74-year-old man is brought in after being found in a confused state by his son. Doctors discover signs of a brain injury, leading his children to reveal the tragedy that befell their father and the shattering effect it has had on his life. A woman who fell down the stairs at her granddaughter's 16th birthday is also in need of treatment, and her daughter is reminded of a phone call that changed their lives forever. Meanwhile, in pediatrics, a talkative nine-year-old is treated for a rash on his ankle.(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Professional duos from across the UK go head to head, aiming to impress judges and celebrated pastry chefs Cherish Finden and Benoit Blin.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almeria, Sharon and Steve attempt to find a property for music lovers Bill and Christine from Hampshire, who enjoy writing songs together and singing them in bars.(n)

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Aussies Karla and Aiden begin the contest in south London with a modern menu inspired by their first rendezvous. Later, Delilah and Laurence cook up a romantic Italian feast, while Jane and John's version of a classic menu sees a catastrophe in the kitchen affect their chances of walking away with the £1,000 prize.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Ex-pats continue chasing their dream lives in the sun, as gite owners in France open a cooking school, a couple from Bristol finally start their businesses and there's a Mexican party to celebrate a successful season in Spain.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin draws on his property expertise to find Dorset couple Andy and Barbara the perfect holiday home in Benidorm on a budget of £150,000. The couple have been together for over 40 years, but after a health scare for Andy, they've decided to seize the moment and buy a bolt-hole in Benidorm, Spain.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Lesley and Phil Ward from north Wales plan to embark on the next chapter of their lives, and hope a full-time move to La Marina Alta on the Costa Blanca in Spain. The pair have a £125,000 budget in mind, and Scarlette Douglas is on hand to show them five properties that might fit the bill, including a home by the beach and a townhouse.(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. First up are Rich and Matt from the Cliff House Hotel, and after tackling Paul and Dave's complaints about the lack of blinds in the bathroom, it's another pair's payment that could prove curtains for their chances in the competition.(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Brighton, the final day of the competition sees social worker Ruth try to impress her guests with her Jewish-Irish fusion menu. There's chaos in the kitchen before the guests even arrive, then after a mixed-up main and some unexpected veggies, Ruth's secret family cheesecake has her guests guessing if it lives up to the bill.(n)

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Alicante, sisters Sophie and Charlotte Rodriguez help former professional dancer Sonia Hart from Somerset find her and husband Martin's perfect holiday home for their budget of 81,000 euros. In Almeria, Sharon Garner and son Shaun search for the perfect love nest for newly engaged couple Will and Gaynor from Kent, within their 175,000-euro budget.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: The shrink is up for a prestigious title at his wine club and offers to host the next tasting in his apartment. Determined to get everything just right, he hires contractors to fix a scratch on the floor - then watches in horror as they wreck the place. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer.(n)

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Part one of two. A miserly pay rise for staff prompts Frasier to cross swords with station manager Kate, but a meeting between the two sees them engage in more than negotiations as emotions are put to the test. Comedy, guest starring Mercedes Ruehl.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Devon, married couple Suzanne and Gary kick off the competition with a menu featuring British classics with an Italian twist. Day two sees mega competitive Tony and Lisa cooking up some simple classics with one too many shop-bought ingredients. Finally it's Nickie and Austen's turn to impress with their 'sloppy but tasty' grub. As the contest hots up, the hosts get a dose of their own medicine when their diners return the criticism they've dished out all week.(n)

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: It's the moment of truth as harvest time arrives for olive farmers in Spain, while plumbing problems threaten the launch of a tea room in France. A Teesside family finds ways to cope when there's a fiesta in Spain.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Married couple Martin and Kate are taking the huge step of buying their first property together overseas - around the Mar Menor in Spain. After a difficult few years they cannot wait to find a holiday bolthole they can enjoy with their two sons Tommy and Charlie for extended breaks. Jean Johansson shows them around five properties in the hope one will be perfect for them and their family with their budget of £90,000.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay gives his advice to Paul Bazzini, an award-winning chef from New York who now owns a restaurant in Ridgewood, New Jersey. Paul confesses he has lost his passion for food and is struggling to keep up with the business, a situation that seems evident to other members of staff, his wife and customers.(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A look back at some of Noel Fitzpatrick's most memorable cases, focusing on some of the extraordinary customised implants and surgeries Noel has performed to save his patients. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A teenager with anorexia nervosa is rushed into the paediatrics emergency department with dangerously low blood pressure. Her mother reflects on her daughter's lifelong struggle with her eating disorder, and the pain she feels at being unable to protect her child. A 66-year-old man who fell from a great height onto a concrete surface is brought in to St George's, and doctors fear he has sustained organ damage and internal bleeding. His daughter talks about their close relationship and the sacrifices her father has made over the years.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay visits Social, an eatery in Costa Mesa, California, that is struggling to succeed in a competitive market. In just 24 hours, Gordon and his team have to revamp the menu and redecorate the premises to create a warm and welcoming environment, while the staff must learn how to work better as a team.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox

programme.title: Celebrity Snoop Dogs

programme.title: The Man with the 10-Stone Testicles
programme.desc: Cameras follow Las Vegas resident Wesley Warren Jr before and after an operation on his giant scrotum, which began swelling in 2008. Doctors refer to his rare condition as scrotal elephantiasis or scrotal lymphedema, characterised by excess watery fluid collecting in tissues of the body. Warren died in 2014 at the age of 49 after suffering multiple heart attacks and had become known around the world for his medical condition.(n)

The programme also revisits a 70-year-old landlady whose tenants stopped paying the rent for a year, and the council's Rogue Landlord Team in Lewisham, who learned where a slum property owner's reputation for a short fuse came from.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Elmore are met with lies and aggression when they track down nearly £3,000 owed by a garage mechanic, while Steve and Ben encounter a retired couple who are being forced to leave their home. Stuart and Vic revisit a young debtor who owe £750 for a laptop computer, and in Oxfordshire, Paul and Steve chase £30,000 owed by a world-famous Formula One racing team.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Agents Stewart and Iain head to Manchester, where a landlord is owed £8,000 in rent arrears and has already called for police back-up. As tempers rise, the pair must find a way to handle a situation that is spiralling dangerously out of control. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase £3,000 owed to a firm of letting agents, before tracking a debtor who was accidentally over-paid for a job. Plus, Stewart and Iain face an eviction that is set to test their patience.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Paul and Ben face a challenging eviction in London, where a vulnerable tenant in rent arrears has barricaded her front door, and the agents receive a tirade of abuse. Meanwhile, Brian and Del chase a debt of nearly £2,000 in Surrey, before trying to recover almost £3,000 in Northamptonshire. Finally, Steve and Ru head to Portsmouth to evict a young family for a landlord who wants her property back.(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Paul finds Emmett safe, but Aaron lies on the incident report to the agency, claiming he was the one to pick up Emmett, and Grant makes a shocking revelation to Mackenzie.(n)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Ryder is closed off following Evan's news, and Maggie inadvertantly pushes Ben and Gemma closer together. Seeing the two of them grow closer proves unwelcome for Nikau.(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Members of the operational support unit of Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit smash through a bedroom window in a drugs raid, a speeding driver is clocked at over 140mph, and friendships are pushed to the limit when amphetamines are found in a car. Plus, vehicle thieves try - and fail - to outpace the interceptors, and an officer gets help from a very unexpected source.(n)

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Sgt Jon Moon and his police dog Kanto follow a trail of broken glass and spilt alcohol, leading to a confrontation with a robber wielding a nasty-looking knife. Elsewhere, a fight in a club ends with one man in handcuffs and another in hospital, Barry Peach plays catch-up as a teenage driver tears up the streets of Skegness, and Jon Peacock detects a whiff of cannabis in a quiet rural village.(n)

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary using eyewitness testimony and footage from camcorders, CCTV and phones to examine traumatic, shocking, humorous and occasionally bizarre experiences of people who have become involved in disputes with neighbours. The programme also reveals how long-fought battles result in Asbos, evictions and sometimes violence.(n)

programme.desc: An Iraqi immigrant kills a female veteran of the second Gulf War for torturing her brother at Abu Ghraib prison. Starring Jesse L Martin and Dennis Farina.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green are convinced that former career criminal Cally Lonegan has killed his business partner who had been stealing company money. However, their suspect has a cast iron alibi - he is a Mob informant and at the time of the murder was being followed by the FBI. The case takes a more complicated turn when an eyewitness is found dead and the detectives discover Lonegan has a twin brother.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the attempted murder of a tough judge, but when the culprit is found Carmichael and McCoy have a difficult time building a case. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach, Sam Waterston and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A rap producer is found shot dead in his studio and suspicion falls on one of his proteges, a hip-hop artist whose lyrics describe a similar killing. Police link a previous murder to the case, but Southerlyn's opinions put her sharply at odds with McCoy as the investigation unfolds. Starring Dennis Farina and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy and Southerlyn battle the lawyers over the fatal shooting of an uninvited nightclub guest who was seen confronting popular rap star Darry 'G-Trane' moments before the murder. Award-winning police drama, starring Sam Waterston and Elisabeth Rohm.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a publishing executive last seen engaged in a heated argument over a taxi. They learn that the killing may have been a race hate crime, with their main suspect having a long history of prejudice. However, a technicality arises in the case, threatening to deny them the conviction. Starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the disappearance of photographer Martin Roe, who was due to testify at an army first lieutenant's court-martial after he captured a picture of what appeared to be a soldier killing an unarmed civilian. However, the photograph is leaked, and Roe is later found dead with a needle in his arm.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs fights for his life on board a US Navy hospital ship after being shot. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and Joanna Teague head for Shanghai, where they hope to take down the leaders of the terrorist group known as The Calling. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Mimi Rogers.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs throws himself back into his work following his near-death experience, updating his appearance and tackling a new risky case alongside DEA agent Luis Mitchell, who wants help bringing down a drugs cartel. Concerned about his colleague's apparent enthusiasm to re-enter the field, DiNozzo discovers Mitchell is the son of a former NCIS agent who gave his life trying to save Gibbs' late wife and daughter, and this attachment is causing Gibbs to break his own rule against personal involvement in a case. However, a significant danger is posed by Benson Long, the cartel's head, and Mitchell's top target.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: One of the NYPD's own is implicated in a cover-up, and the SVU tries to unravel a complex sex trafficking operation with roots in government offices, the police department and the Catholic Church. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Racial tensions begin to boil over after an unarmed black man is shot by police from another precinct, and three officers are put on trial.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A man is found stabbed to death in an alleyway, and the detectives discover he shined shoes for a living. They begin by looking for his most recent customers, and learn the victim was a Vietnam war veteran, shedding light on possible motives and suspects.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Fontana and Green are baffled by a series of flu-related deaths until they discover the victims were given bogus vaccines. McCoy sets out to prosecute the man responsible for their distribution, but a mistake by new ADA Alexandra Borgia threatens their case and leaves the rookie at odds with her boss.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate when a priest is found murdered in the confessional booth. The arrest of a teenager with a grudge seems to have them on the right track, but after discovering that the clergyman wasn't the killer's intended target they begin to focus their search on a petrol station cashier. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A high-level insurance executive is murdered, and suspicion soon falls on the father of a dying nine-year-old whose claim for a life-saving leukaemia treatment was turned down. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs travels to his home town after hearing news of his father's death, while the team investigates whether a fire on a US Navy ship was accidental or planned. The vessel served as prison to several members of the Brotherhood of Doubt terrorist group, whom Gibbs was responsible for catching. After the blaze, two of those members are missing. Tony argues that Gibbs would want to know, but Vance does not want him disturbed at this difficult time. Mark Harmon stars.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs fights for his life on board a US Navy hospital ship after being shot. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and Joanna Teague head for Shanghai, where they hope to take down the leaders of the terrorist group known as The Calling. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Mimi Rogers.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Marine is found murdered in Quantico, mere hours after calling Gibbs to discuss a possible case. In response, Bishop and McGee go undercover as a married couple to run surveillance on a lieutenant and his wife, who may be connected to the death. Starring Mark Harmon, Emily Wickersham and Michael Weatherly.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A young Navy Seaman is found dead, with his eyes gouged out, after being involved in a car accident. Director Vance makes a rare return to field duty, pairing up with Gibbs to investigate when it becomes clear the killing seems to be connected to the case of Kip Klugman, a former NCIS agent he once locked up for stealing evidence. Starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll.(n)

programme.title: Private Eyes

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team tries to prevent a miscarriage of justice after a young model is raped by a renowned fashion photographer, who looks to his successful brother to make the charges disappear. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hartigay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The Bakers, the stars of America's top reality TV show, are rocked by scandal when 13-year-old daughter Lane is discovered to be three months pregnant. Ultra-conservative parents Pam and Frank resist SVU involvement in a misguided attempt to shield their 10 children - and their lucrative TV empire - from disgrace. However, when Lane accuses one of the show's cameramen of raping her, one who happens to have already had a vasectomy, it becomes clear the Bakers are trying to protect someone. Unable to aid in the inquiry due to her own pregnancy, Rollins decides to make good use of her time away from the beat to binge-watch the Bakers' show in an effort to identify potential suspects. Crime drama, starring Mariska Hartigay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The hunt for a missing teenage girl takes an emotional toll on Sgt Dodds, and DNA evidence becomes the crucial link the team needs to identify the youngster's abductor. programme.title: The Slipper and the Rose (1976)
programme.desc: A girl is reduced to being the downtrodden servant of her cruel stepmother after her father's death. With a little help from her fairy godmother, she wins the heart of a handsome prince, who is desperately trying to avoid being forced into a political marriage by his parents. Musical version of Cinderella, starring Gemma Craven and Richard Chamberlain.(n)

programme.desc: Two boys become separated from the rest of their class while on a trip to a small island - and end up getting captured by a gang of armed criminals. Adventure, starring Michael Elphick, Martin Murphy and Michael Salmon.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Meet the Lords
programme.desc: Documentary filmed over a year following life at the House of Lords, one of Britain's oldest, most idiosyncratic and most important institutions, going behind the scenes during a turbulent 12 months that has seen dramatic changes in the political landscape and the Lords battling it out with the Government. The first edition introduces the larger-than-life characters that populate the House, including new people's peers, hereditaries and political grandees, as they try to change legislation and follow their personal passions.(n)

programme.title: Fox
programme.desc: Joey tries to settle the problem with Liz, and Kenny prepares for the most important fight of his career. Guest starring Patricia Quinn (The Rocky Horror Picture Show).(n)

programme.title: Mad Dog Morgan (1976)
programme.desc: An Irishman seeking his fortune in the Australian Gold Rush ends up in prison when driven to robbery out of desperation. After 12 years of abuse, he is released and becomes an outlaw, determined to make society pay for his suffering - and becoming a hero to the downtrodden in the process. Fact-based Western, starring Dennis Hopper and Jack Thompson.(n)

programme.title: Golden Years (2016)
programme.desc: A retired couple face a grim future after a financial crisis costs them both their pensions. Refusing to take the situation lying down, they resolve to take back what is rightfully theirs with a series of daring bank robberies. Comedy, starring Bernard Hill, Virginia McKenna, Simon Callow and Sue Johnston.(n)

programme.title: Closing the Ring (2007)
programme.desc: An old woman consumed by grief following the death of her husband receives a message from a stranger who claims to have found a ring belonging to her that was lost decades before. The discovery brings to light secrets from her past and causes her to look back to the beginnings of her marriage. Drama, starring Shirley MacLaine, Christopher Plummer and Neve Campbell.(n)

programme.title: Black Island (1978)

programme.title: Anoop and The Elephant (1972)
programme.desc: Two English children meet an Indian boy and his pet elephant, and help to keep the animal out of the hands of a greedy circus owner. Family drama, with Anoop Singh and Phil Daniels.(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Choice (1963)
programme.desc: A married woman finds herself caught in a moral dilemma when children inadvertently cause the death of her diabetic lover. Drama, starring Anthony Steel, Jeanne Moody and Ballard Berkeley.(n)

programme.title: A Handful of Dust (1989)
programme.desc: A couple's life is disrupted by a penniless woman who arrives for the weekend, setting in motion a disastrous sequence of events. Drama, starring Judi Dench, whose performance won her a Bafta award, and James Wilby.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Documentary exploring areas of the capital, beginning with Shooters Hill, Charlton, Greenwich and Blackheath.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks above and alongside two large bodies of water - Ladybower Reservoir and Windermere.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Amanda Lamb helps a couple search for a dream holiday home on the Greek islands of Paros and Naxos, including a dwelling on the beach, a chic urban home and a beautiful farmhouse with seven stunning sun-terraces.(n)

programme.title: The Bionic Vet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick prepares to amputate a corgi's cancerous paw and replace it with a prosthetic foot, a procedure that has only been carried out on two other dogs. He also catches up with a puppy that was suffering from a painful bone deformity, and treats a barn owl brought into the surgery with a broken wing.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Canary Wharf and Southbank. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The Second World War saw men from all classes unite to fight the Nazis, and this programme meets some of the last surviving pilots of the Battle of Britain. Women, who had been excluded from many jobs, worked in everything from anti-aircraft gun batteries to steel-making factories. The Blitz saw rescue workers risk all to save those trapped in bombed buildings. For many of the era's last survivors, the defiant spirit that brought the nation together remains one of their proudest moments, as this documentary reveals.(n)

programme.title: Oxford Street Revealed
programme.desc: The British Transport Police deal with an injured passenger and two disgruntled tourists. Meanwhile, a team of miners aims to prevent a billion-pound London Underground project from hitting the buffers, and a couple celebrate their first anniversary.(n)

programme.title: The Repo Man
programme.desc: In recession-hit Britain, many people have turned to finance companies to purchase items over a fixed period of time. This documentary follows the life and work of bailiff Sean James as he confronts debtors across the nation who fail to keep up their repayments.(n)

programme.title: Pirates of Somalia (2017)
programme.desc: In 2008, rookie journalist Jay Bahadur forms a plan to embed himself among the pirates of Somalia, and provides a rare profile of these men and the forces that drive them. Fact-based drama, starring Al Pacino and Evan Peters.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman.(n)
